fundamentals.name: Meta Platforms, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Meta Platforms, Inc. engages in the development of products that enable people to connect and share with friends and family through mobile devices, personal computers, virtual reality (VR) headsets, and AI glasses in the United States, Canada, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and internationally. It operates through two segments, Family of Apps (FoA) and Reality Labs (RL). The FoA segment offers Facebook, which enables people to build community through feed, reels, stories, groups, marketplace, and other; Instagram that brings people closer through Instagram feed, stories, reels, live, and messaging; Messenger, a messaging application for people to connect with friends, family, communities, and businesses across platforms and devices through text, audio, and video calls; Meta AI, an assistant that's available across apps, as a stand-alone app, on AI glasses, and on the web; Threads, an application for text-based updates and public conversations; and WhatsApp, a messaging application that is used by people and businesses to communicate and transact. The RL segment provides virtual and augmented reality products, including consumer hardware, software, and content that help people feel connected, as well as Meta Quest devices that enable social experiences across gaming, fitness, entertainment, and more. The segment also includes wearables such as AI glasses like Ray Ban Meta and Oakley Meta glasses; and the Meta Ray Ban Display, which combines AI glasses with an integrated lens display and the Meta Neural Band, a wrist worn device using electromyography that lets people control their AI glasses through neuromuscular signals. Meta Platforms, Inc. has a collaboration with Microsoft Corporation, NVIDIA Corporation, Advanced Micro Devices, Inc., Broadcom Inc., and OpenAI, L.L.C. The company was formerly known as Facebook, Inc. and changed its name to Meta Platforms, Inc. in October 2021. The company was incorporated in 2004 and is headquartered in Menlo Park, California.

judgment.summary: Meta Platforms demonstrates robust growth, high profitability, and a strong balance sheet, trading at a reasonable forward valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 56.8%; next earnings date is 2026-07-29.
positives: Revenue growth 33.1% YoY and earnings growth 62.4% YoY indicate strong top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Operating margin 40.6% and profit margin 32.8% highlight exceptional profitability.
Forward P/E of 16.92 and PEG ratio of 0.88 suggest the stock is attractively valued relative to its growth prospects.
Free cash flow of $25.56B TTM provides significant financial flexibility.
Analyst consensus is a strong buy with a mean target of $826.60.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 35.61, while not excessive, indicates some leverage.
The Supreme Court's decision not to hear the Vermont lawsuit presents a potential legal overhang.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter (5.4%) and fiscal year (8.9%) are lower than recent historical growth rates.

news.paragraph: Meta Platforms is reportedly one of the most undervalued big tech stocks, with a recent analyst target revision from Wells Fargo and inclusion in a group of tech stocks driving market excitement. The company is also leveraging AI to reshape its workforce, though it faces a setback as the US Supreme Court declined to hear its challenge to a social media addiction lawsuit from Vermont. Despite the legal news, the overall sentiment from analysts and market commentary remains positive.

headlines.title: Meta Platforms May Be the Most Undervalued Big Tech Stock in the Market
US Supreme Court won't hear Meta's challenge to Vermont social media addiction lawsuit
Google, Meta, Amazon, and 6 other tech stocks powering the stock market's profit excitement
Meta Leans on AI to Reshape Workforce
Wells Fargo revises Meta stock price target for rest of 2026
